RE: Conflict of interest concernj-ng participation in adopting
the Standish-Litchfield Fire Protection Districts annual
Budget.
Dear Sirs:
In reply to your request for a written letter from Board
of Directors Chairman Arnold Reddoch, concerning his
participation in the adoption of our new 1992-1993 budget.
Mr. Reddoch is concerned with the matter of our line item
#25 (transportation and travel). He does not wish to participate
in approving this line itemr dS the department has in the past
purchased fuel for our fire equipment from his establishment.
As it was stated by your office to Mona Ingram the Standish
Litchfield Fire Protection District Secretary, when she called
your office on Mr. Reddochs request, that he should not
participate in any monetary decision that affects this particular
line item #25.
Mr. Reddoch would appreciate a letter from your office
for his files, and for the Districts files concerning your
decision.
We are including a photocopy of our expenditure qruideline
concerning this item in guestion, and we are also including
a photocopy of Mr. Reddoch's letter which he received from our
Lassen County Counsel, Mr. Dawson Arnold.
Thanking you kindly, f am -___---

Attention: Mr. Arnold Reddoch, Chairman
RE: Request for Conflict of Interest Opinion
Dear Mr. Reddoch and Members of the Board of Directors:
By hand-delivered letter recei.ved May 21 , L992, you request
a written opinion of this office as to whether the purchase of
diesel and gasoline by the oistrict from wayside Inn, in which
Arnold Reddoch, one of the District Directors, has a proprietary
interest, is a "conflict of interest" prohibited by Calitornia 1aw.
Our conclusion is in the negative.
There are two sets of "conflict of interest" provisions in
California 1aw. One such set of provisions, contained in the
Political Reform Act, ( Government Code S 81000 et seq. ) deals with
disclosure of the interests of public officers and employees and
their disqualification from participating in decisions which woutd
affect their private interests. The second set of provisions is
contained in what is known as the Corrupt Practices Act,
( Government Code SS 1090 through 1097, inclusive). section 1o9o
provides that public officers or employees sha11 not be financially
j-nterested in any contract made by them in their official capacity
or by any body or board of which they are members and that such
officers and employees sha11 not be a vendor at any purchase rnade
by them in their official capacity.
A list of exceptions is 
provided to said prohibition in
Government Code S 1091 rrhich reads in pertinent part as follows:
(a) An officer shall not be deemed to be
interested in a contract entered into by a body
or board of which the officer is a member
within the meaning of this article if the
officer has only a remote interest in the
..f trrt.B Bgz


Arnold Reddoch
Page 2

contract and if the fact of that i[telest i-s
disclosed to the body of the board of which
the officer is a nenber and [oted in its
official records, and thereafter the body or
board authorizes, approves, or ratifies the
contract in good faith. by a vote of its
nembership sufficient for the purpose lrithout
counting the vote or votes of the office! or
nenber with the remote interest.
(b) as used in this article, 
"renote
ilterest" neans any of the followi.ng:
(1)...

(8) That of a supplier of goods or
services l{helr those goods or services have been
supplied to the contlactj.ng party by the
officer for at least five years prior to his
or he! election or appointnent to office...
rd.
I have personally reviewed pertinent lecolds in the
offj-ce of the Lassen county clerk which reflect that virgil A. and
wanda L. Reddoch aDd uarvin w. Dye and Anita M. Dye fj-led a
statenent of doing business under the fictitious name "wayside Inn"
on April 15, 1981. The county clerk's records for public officers
rithin the County of Lassen reflect that Arnold Reddoch was
appointed to the office of Director of the standish-Litchfield Fire
Protection District on Novenber 14, 1989. Thus, as Mr, Reddoch has
been a supplier of goods to the District for more than fj-ve years
prior to his assunption of office, his interest is defined as a
"remote intelest" and the District may contj-nue to purchase its
fuel fron the wayside Inn.
As stated above, Mr. Reddoch should not palticipate in
approval of any bill or varrant lthich involves the pulchase of fuel
fron the wayside Inn, as he is disqualified under lhe provisions
of covernment Code S a?100 ("no public offj.cial at any tevel of
state or 1ocal goverrunent sha1l rnake, participate in nakiug, or in
any way attenpt to use his officiat position to influence a
govelnmental decision in vhich he knows or has reason to know he
has a financiat interest.") I'lr. Reddoch must al.so ensure that his
abstention, and the reason fo! il, is duly noted in the ninutes of
the board neeting as required by S 1091(a).


Arnold Reddoch
Page 3
l{ay 2a, 1992
I lecotnnend lhat Mr. Reddoch contact directty the Fai!
Potiticat Practices comnission at 428 J street, suite 6oo,
sacranento, cal.ifolnia 95814, (916) 322-5660, and request advise
from that body as to vhether he nay participate in the adoption
of the anlua1 district budget (as the budget doubtless includes a
IiIle item for "fue]." which rnight reasonably be expected to be
purchased fron his business. This office can offer no advice on
that question as the "advice of counsel" defense in colflict of
interest nalters ties sol-ely within the jurisdiction of the FPPC.
and alises onLy fron fornal opinions issued by it.
one final connent is in order. You! letter notes that I'lr.
Reddoch has been appointed Board chairrnan. whil.e I have no
infornation on what is the plocedure in the standish-Litchfield
Fire Protection Dj,strict by-l.aws of sone districts provide that the
chairnan signs various official docrments including r[inutes and or
warrants. other districts have all the nenbers of the boald sign
ninutes as approved. In any event, the act of affixing the
signature of the Chair, or President of the Board, is ninisterial
in nature only and it is the opinion of this office that the coulls
lrould not regald the act of s.igning papers as either "participating
in a decision" or as entering into a contract with himself vithin
the neaning of either the PoliticaL Reform Act or Corrupt Practices
Law.
I sincerely trust that this letter wilL be dispositive of the
matter, but if further infornation is desired, please do not
hesitate to contact me.
sincerely.

sections.question_synthetic: The Standish-Litchfield Fire Protection District seeks FPPC written confirmation regarding Board Chairman Arnold Reddoch's conflict of interest concerning his participation in approving the district's annual budget, particularly line item #25 (transportation and travel), given that the district has purchased fuel from Reddoch's business (Wayside Inn).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request to FPPC (not an FPPC response letter), plus supporting documentation including a county counsel opinion. The county counsel opinion provides analysis but explicitly states that FPPC formal advice is needed. No Q/C sections in the incoming request letter itself. Confidence reduced due to document being a compilation of materials rather than a single coherent FPPC advice letter.

embedding.summary: This is a compilation of correspondence from the Standish-Litchfield Fire Protection District to the FPPC requesting written advice on a conflict of interest matter, along with a prior opinion letter from Lassen County Counsel concluding that Reddoch's interest is 'remote' under Government Code §1091(b)(8), but recommending direct FPPC consultation regarding budget participation under the Political Reform Act.
